# Contributing

Thank you for your interest in contributing to webmcp.land!

The following is a set of guidelines for contributing to this project. These are
mostly guidelines, not strict rules. Use your best judgment and feel free to
propose changes to this document in a pull request.

## Code of Conduct

This project and everyone participating in it is governed by our
[Code of Conduct](./CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md). By participating, you are expected to
uphold this code.

## How Can I Contribute?

### Adding New Prompts

The easiest way to contribute a prompt is through the website:

1. Visit [webmcp.land](https://webmcp.land)
2. Login with GitHub
3. Create your prompt using the prompt editor
4. Submit — your prompt will be reviewed and merged automatically

### Reporting Issues

- Check the GitHub Issues first to avoid duplicating an existing issue.
- Use the issue tracker to ask questions, report problems or for discussion
  related to the project.

### Submitting a Pull Request

- Fork the repo
- Make your changes in a new branch
- Provide tests for your changes, particularly for new features or fixes
- Push changes to your own fork
- Submit a pull request, linking it to the relevant issue
